summary: Iter-31 testing of (P2) Shipper Saved Payment Methods + (P3) Finnish/English i18n on the shipper-tracking screen. BACKEND: 9/9 pytest cases in test_iter31_payment_methods.py pass. All review-request bullets verified — auth guard (401), GET /api/shipper/payment-methods returns {customer_id, payment_methods:[]} initially, POST /api/shipper/payment-methods/setup-checkout returns a real https://checkout.stripe.com/c/pay/cs_test_... URL + cs_ session id, stripe_customer_id is lazily created and persisted on the shipper doc (cus_UhEVtqHTW7BcS3), attaching pm_card_visa via Stripe SDK then GET shows brand=visa last4=4242, POST /default flips is_default=true, DELETE detaches the card and it disappears from the list, and the ownership guard returns 403 for a pm attached to a different Stripe customer (pm_card_amex test). FRONTEND: /shipper-payment-methods screen renders perfectly on mobile viewport (390x844) — title 'Payment methods', subtitle 'Save a card for faster checkout. Your card details are stored securely by Stripe.', the seeded Visa •••• 4242 card with 'Make default' button + delete (trash) button, the 'Add a card' button (testID=payment-methods-add) and 'Secured by Stripe' footer all visible. Tapping the 'Add a card' button calls POST /api/shipper/payment-methods/setup-checkout and the browser redirects to https://checkout.stripe.com/c/pay/cs_test_... ✅. i18n on /shipper-tracking VERIFIED — switching localStorage('@nadaruns_language')='fi' renders 'Maksu', 'Reitin tiedot', 'Paketin tiedot', 'Toimitettu' (Finnish), switching to 'en' renders 'Payment', 'Route Details', 'Package Details'. NO raw i18n keys (e.g. 'tracking.routeDetails') leak. Other tracking labels (yourDriver, verificationCodes, timeline) are conditionally rendered and didn't appear on the chosen test shipment scroll viewport, but their t() bindings exist at shipper-tracking.tsx:480/516/611/632/657/686 and the en/fi JSON have all keys at en.json:492+, fi.json:492+, so they will render correctly when their parent conditions are met. Regression: /shipper-create wizard still loads.
action_items: (NONE-BLOCKING) The shipper-settings screen briefly shows the driver-only fallback ('Please login as a driver') when navigated directly via deep link before AuthContext has rehydrated user.type=='shipper' from localStorage. This was observed under a synthetic localStorage-bootstrap test; it does NOT reproduce in the normal login → shipper-home → shipper-settings UX (verified in iter-30). No fix required.
critical_code_review_comments: /app/backend/services/payments.py — create_setup_checkout_session uses mode='setup' + payment_method_types=['card'] correctly. list_payment_methods reads invoice_settings.default_payment_method and marks is_default; sorts default first. Good.
/app/backend/server.py:5993-6010 _get_or_create_shipper_customer lazily creates the customer and persists stripe_customer_id on the shipper doc. Idempotent (reads existing).

/app/backend/server.py:6060-6072 _assert_pm_owned correctly raises 403 when pm.customer != customer_id. Properly catches 404 from Stripe.retrieve. Ownership guard verified by test (pm_card_amex attached to a different customer returns 403 for both POST /default and DELETE).

/app/frontend/app/shipper-payment-methods.tsx — clean: handles web (window.location.href = url) and native (WebBrowser.openAuthSessionAsync) branches for the Stripe Checkout setup URL; refreshes list on focus; proper testIDs (payment-methods-back, payment-methods-add, payment-method-{id}, payment-method-default-{id}, payment-method-delete-{id}); 333 lines, well under cap.

/app/frontend/app/shipper-settings.tsx:316-320 — Payment methods row correctly wires testID='shipper-payment-methods-link' and router.push('/shipper-payment-methods'). Casts `as any` to placate Expo Router's strict route typing; fine.

/app/frontend/src/i18n/locales/en.json & fi.json — tracking.* and paymentMethods.* trees are complete and aligned (same keys in both languages). No raw key leakage observed in tracking screen for either locale.

success_rate.frontend: 100% (Payment Methods screen + add-card → Stripe redirect + EN/FI i18n on tracking all verified)

seed_data_creation: Reused demo.shipper@nadaruns.com. Stripe customer cus_UhEVtqHTW7BcS3 lazily created. pytest attaches/detaches pm_card_visa and creates/cleans up a throwaway 'other@test.local' Stripe customer for the ownership-guard test. One pm_card_visa was left attached during browser test (visible as '•••• 4242' on the Payment methods screen) — harmless test artefact.
context_for_next_testing_agent: (1) Backend test STRIPE_API_KEY must come from /app/backend/.env, NOT os.environ.get — the container env carries STRIPE_API_KEY=sk_test_emergent placeholder which is not a real key. Test fixture stripe_key now reads .env first. (2) i18n storage key is '@nadaruns_language' (AsyncStorage); set this in localStorage before page.goto for the language to take effect on web. (3) Shipper auth bootstrap from localStorage: keys are 'nadaruns_auth_token' (raw JWT string) and 'nadaruns_user' (JSON with at minimum {id, email, type:'shipper'}). When deep-linking to /shipper-settings directly with this bootstrap, AuthContext may briefly show a driver fallback before hydrating; this does NOT reproduce via normal login flow. (4) For payment-methods CRUD, attach a Stripe pm via the SDK to the shipper's customer (GET /api/shipper/payment-methods to obtain customer_id) rather than completing the hosted Checkout form.
rca_of_issue: No new bugs. Initial backend pytest failure was a test-side env issue (the container's STRIPE_API_KEY env var contained the placeholder 'sk_test_emergent'); fixed by preferring the value from /app/backend/.env in the fixture. After that, 9/9 pass.
